description.value: A hollow tube filled with small pebbles that mimic the sound of rain when tilted.

description.value:
Wondrous item

This prism has 10 charges. While you are holding it, you can use an action to speak one of three command words to cause one of the following effects:

  • The first command word causes the gem to shed bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. This effect doesn't expend a charge. It lasts until you use a bonus action to repeat the command word or until you use another function of the gem.
  • The second command word expends 1 charge and causes the gem to fire a brilliant beam of light at one creature you can see within 60 feet of you. The creature must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or become blinded for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
  • The third command word expends 5 charges and causes the gem to flare with blinding light in a 30- foot cone originating from it. Each creature in the cone must make a saving throw as if struck by the beam created with the second command word.

When all of the gem's charges are expended, the gem becomes a nonmagical jewel worth 50 gp.

Value with 10 charges: 50GP

unidentified.description: This faceted crystal shines with an inner light, casting subtle, mesmerizing patterns of color around it. It feels warm to the touch, and its glow seems to brighten in dark areas.
unidentified.name: Radiant Crystal

description.value:
A vibrant, multicolored feather from a rare jungle bird.

The feather's colors shift and change in the light, creating a dazzling display. It is remarkably light and delicate, yet it seems almost indestructible.

gm-notes.notes:
Intrigue: The vendor tells tales of this feather granting charisma and influence to its bearer, making it a prized possession for those seeking to sway others.


GM Notes (Magical): The feather grants the user advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks for 24 hours when tucked into a hat or worn in the hair.

description.value: This armor is leather armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes. Ring mail is inferior to chain mail, and it's usually worn only by those who can't afford better armor.

description.value:
Wondrous item

This tiny object looks like a feather. Different types of feather tokens exist, each with a different single-use effect. The GM chooses the kind of token or determines it randomly from the @UUID[Compendium.dnd5e.tables.RollTable.OEdr086W61t1DRJo]{Feather Token} table.

Swan Boat. You can use an action to touch the token to a body of water at least 60 feet in diameter. The token disappears, and a 50-foot-long, 20-foot- wide boat shaped like a swan takes its place. The boat is self-propelled and moves across water at a speed of 6 miles per hour. You can use an action while on the boat to command it to move or to turn up to 90 degrees. The boat can carry up to thirty-two Medium or smaller creatures. A Large creature counts as four Medium creatures, while a Huge creature counts as nine. The boat remains for 24 hours and then disappears. You can dismiss the boat as an action.

Any hit with this item causes the target to suffer from bleeding, taking 1d4 damage at the start of each of its turns until it uses an action to staunch the wound. The item is lightweight, weighing 25% less than standard counterparts.

A thick shaft of wood wrapped with a sturdy grip makes a highly functional weapon in addition to a stout walking stick.

This item is crafted from hardened bamboo, known for its lightweight and flexibility.

This item is crafted from hardened bamboo, known for its lightweight and flexibility.

It provides a +3 bonus to initiative rolls and a +2 bonus to Dexterity checks and saving throws as long as it is equipped and actively used in combat.

If the DM feels the weapon is not being used actively enough in combat, these bonuses will be removed.

However, it is weak against hard surfaces, causing the wielder to have disadvantage on attack rolls against targets with natural armor or wearing metal armor.

Bamboo sap provides some protection against insects.

description.value:
The One-Time Use Immovable Rod is a marvel of magical engineering, designed to provide a single use of an otherwise extraordinary effect. This rod is crafted from a smooth, dark metal and is approximately one foot long. It has a small, red button on one end and intricate, arcane runes engraved along its length.

When the button is pressed, the rod emits a faint, blue glow and locks itself in place, defying gravity and remaining immovable in the air or against any solid surface. This effect persists until the rod is deactivated or its magical energy is expended.

Mechanics:

  • Activation: Pressing the button on the end of the rod activates its immovable property. The rod can then hold up to 8,000 pounds of weight and remains fixed in space.
  • Duration: The rod remains immovable until the button is pressed again to deactivate it or until its single use is consumed. Once used and deactivated, the rod loses its magical properties permanently.
  • Special Use: The rod can be used to create a stable anchor point in mid-air, secure objects or creatures in place, or even as a makeshift step or handle in tricky situations.
  • Limitations: This version of the Immovable Rod is designed for one-time use only. After being activated and then deactivated, the rod's magic is expended, and it functions as a mundane metal rod.

Flavor Text: "This seemingly ordinary metal rod holds a remarkable enchantment, allowing it to defy gravity and stay firmly in place with the press of a button. Perfect for securing gear, creating an impromptu ladder, or holding a door shut, the One-Time Use Immovable Rod offers adventurers a versatile tool for critical moments. However, its magic is fleeting, so use it wisely."

unidentified.description:
This plain, metal rod has a small button on one end and feels unusually heavy for its size. When the button is pressed, the rod emits a slight hum and resists any movement.

Its mysterious properties suggest it could be used to stabilize objects or even people in place.
unidentified.name: Rigid Baton

Shiny, black berries that grow on creeping vines in dark, secluded areas.

They are highly toxic if ingested raw but have powerful alchemical properties when processed correctly. These berries are essential for making oils that ensure slipperiness.

description.value: These fist-sized nuts grow on wukka trees, which are popular haunts for jaculi, su-monsters, and zorbos. This magical light fades after 1 minute, but shaking the nut again causes the light to reappear. If the shell of the nut is cracked open, it loses its magic.

An old, worn coin with strange, indecipherable markings. The metal is tarnished and rough, but it holds a certain mystique.

The coin feels oddly heavy for its size, and there's a faint aura of something ancient about it.

gm-notes.notes: Intrigue: The vendor insists it’s a key to an ancient treasure, and many have tried to decode its secrets without success.

This item is crafted from jaguar hide, known for its stealth-enhancing properties.

It provides a +1 bonus to Dexterity (Stealth) checks, a +1 bonus to saving throws against environmental hazards.

However, it provides 1 less AC compared to standard leather or studded leather armor.

The pendant is attached to a fine gold chain.

gm-notes.notes: Intrigue: This pendant is said to bring protection and good fortune to its wearer. It also has a hidden compartment that can hold a tiny rolled-up piece of paper.

A magical flask that keeps any liquid inside cool and refreshing.

Drinking from this flask grants a +2 bonus on Constitution saving throws against heat for 1 hour.

description.value:
Delicate, star-shaped flowers with shimmering petals that change color with the light.

They grow in high-altitude regions and are prized for their mystical properties. These flowers are key to crafting potions that grant clairvoyance.

This brilliantly colored beetle shell glows faintly in the dark, emitting a soft, otherworldly light. The iridescent colors shift and change as you move it, creating a mesmerizing effect. It feels surprisingly warm to the touch.

gm-notes.notes: Intrigue: Said to be from a species of beetle that only appears under the light of a full moon, this shell is believed to bring good luck to its owner.

description.value:
This heavy cloth bag is adorned with vibrant, intricate patterns of beans in various stages of growth. Inside, you find 1d3 dry beans, each slightly different in shape and color. The bag itself feels slightly warm to the touch, as if imbued with an ancient and mysterious magic.

Mystery and Usage:

  • Planting and Growth: If a single bean is removed from the bag, planted in dirt or sand, and then watered, it will produce a magical effect one minute later from the ground where it was planted. The specific effect can only be discovered through this process, adding an element of surprise and wonder to the experience.

Flavor Text: "The Enchanted Pouch of Mystical Beans seems to hum with latent energy, each bean promising a hidden potential. The ancient magic woven into the fabric of this pouch and the beans within it resists all but the most profound understanding. To unlock their secrets, one must dare to plant and nurture them, trusting in the capricious nature of their magic."

magic-items-2.destroyFlavorText: reaches 0 charges: it crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

gm-notes.notes:
The GM can choose an effect from the @UUID[Compendium.dnd5e.tables.RollTable.4ryVFV5LPHPzRKxl]{Bag of Beans} table, determine it randomly, or create an effect.

 

Special Note: Despite its enchanting appearance, the true nature of the beans inside this pouch remains a mystery. Attempts to identify the beans using magic such as the Identify spell reveal only that they are enchanted with unpredictable magic, leaving their true potential hidden until they are planted and watered. The magic within the beans is ancient and inscrutable, resisting all attempts to fully understand its properties through conventional means.

description.value: This item is crafted from Dwarven Steel, known for its resilience and strength. It provides a +1 bonus to damage rolls and advantage on checks to avoid being disarmed.

description.value: A large fish scale that shimmers with all the colors of the rainbow. It is smooth and slightly curved, with a faintly iridescent quality that catches the light beautifully.

gm-notes.notes:
Intrigue: The vendor insists that this scale holds the power of the ocean, making it a prized possession for those who seek the sea's blessings.


GM Notes (Magical): Grants the user the ability to breathe underwater for 1 hour once per day.

description.value:
This item is crafted from ironwood, known for its durability and strength.

It provides a +1 bonus to AC with +1 bonus to Strength checks and saving throws

However, it is heavy, imposing a -5 penalty to movement speed and a -1 to Dex checks.

The item also provides good protection against undergrowth hazards like thorns and vines.

description.value:
A miniature hourglass with sand that glows faintly. The frame is made of a dark, polished wood, and the glass is clear and smooth.

The sand inside falls at varying speeds, sometimes faster, sometimes slower.

gm-notes.notes: Intrigue: The vendor claims the hourglass can reveal hidden truths about the passage of time, though its exact properties remain a mystery.

description.value:
These berries are dark brown and bitter. A full-grown sinda berry bush has 4d6 berries growing on it. A bush plucked of all its berries grows new berries in 1d4 months. Picked berries lose their freshness and efficacy after 24 hours.

Fresh sinda berries can be eaten raw or crushed and added to a drink to dull the bitterness. A creature that consumes at least ten fresh sinda berries gains advantage on saving throws against disease and poison for the next 24 hours.

magic-items-2.destroyFlavorText: reaches 0 charges: it crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

description.value: A candle that, once lit, never seems to melt or go out. The flame flickers with a soft, steady glow, providing light without heat.

gm-notes.notes:
Intrigue: Perfect for exploring dark places without the risk of fire, this candle is a valuable tool for adventurers.
GM Notes (Magical): Provides light without heat, making it ideal for safe exploration.

description.value: Tall, thin stalks topped with glowing, ethereal bulbs. These plants thrive in marshy areas and emit a soft, flickering light at night, resembling will-o'-the-wisps. They are used to create potions that grant the ability to become invisible.
Glasses designed to help with reading maps and other fine details.

description.value:
A small totem pole carved from dark wood, depicting various jungle animals and spirits. The carvings are detailed and painted with natural dyes.

Notes: Tribal elders and spiritual leaders may offer more for this item, seeing it as a piece of cultural heritage.

Any creature that ingests a ryath root gains 2d4 temporary hit points. A creature that consumes more than one ryath root in a 24-hour period must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or suffer the poisoned condition for 1 hour.

magic-items-2.destroyFlavorText: reaches 0 charges: it crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

description.value: This tiny elephant figurine is expertly carved from jade, its surface smooth and cool to the touch. Despite its small size, the craftsmanship is exquisite, capturing the majesty of the creature in minute detail. It fits perfectly in the palm of your hand.

gm-notes.notes: Intrigue: Believed to bring good fortune to those who carry it, this figurine is a popular token among travelers and merchants.

description.value:
This black, tar-like liquid is harvested from undead creatures by unscrupulous necromancers and dark priests. Essentially distilled blood of an undead creature, Rot smells bad and tastes worse.

The dark liquid, which is sold in small vials, carry traces of the necromantic energy that fuels the undead, allowing anyone to gain a brief taste of an undead creature's numbed existence.

Despite its foul nature, Rot is very popular among street beggars and vagrants, who use it to keep pain and hunger at bay. Rot-users are easily recognizable by their grayish skin, dead eyes and often ghastly smiles. Long-time users also show signs of hair loss, decaying teeth and general muscle atrophy.

Rot is also known as 'ghoul's blood' or 'grave drink'. Users often refer to the consumption of rot as 'rotting' or 'gravecrawling'.

Dose. An ounce of dark liquid. A single dose of Rot costs around 25 gp, while a small flask containing 10 doses sells for around 200 gp.

Use. You can drink a dose of Rot as an action.

Effect. Immediately after consuming a dose of Rot, you begin to feel numbed in both mind and spirit. For 8 hours, you have a +4 bonus to your Constitution score and you don't need to breathe, sleep, drink or eat. You also have disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws and ability checks.

magic-items-2.destroyFlavorText: reaches 0 charges: it crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

gm-notes.notes:
Overdosing. For each dose beyond the first, you must 
succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or suffer a 
level of exhaustion. 


Withdrawal. When the effect ends, you must make 
a Constitution saving throw against a DC of 15 + the 
number of doses of Rot you've consumed. On a failure, 
your Constitution score is permanently reduced by 1. If 
this causes your Constitution score to become 0, you die 
and rise

description.value:
(Requires attunement by a cleric, druid, or warlock)

You can use an action to speak this staff's command word and throw the staff on the ground within 10 feet of you. The staff becomes a @UUID[Compendium.dnd5e.monsters.Actor.NpvwE1feOHyEqAbP]{Giant Constrictor Snake} under your control and acts on its own initiative count. By using a bonus action to speak the command word again, you return the staff to its normal form in a space formerly occupied by the snake.

On your turn, you can mentally command the snake if it is within 60 feet of you and you aren't incapacitated. You decide what action the snake takes and where it moves during its next turn, or you can issue it a general command, such as to attack your enemies or guard a location.

If the snake is reduced to 0 hit points, it dies and reverts to its staff form. The staff then shatters and is destroyed. If the snake reverts to staff form before losing all its hit points, it regains all of them.

description.value:
This life-sized statue of a jaguar is carved from a single block of obsidian, its surface polished to a mirror-like finish.

The jaguar's eyes are set with large emeralds that seem to glow with an inner light. The statue is incredibly detailed, capturing the sleek muscles and fierce expression of the jungle predator. It sits poised as if ready to pounce, radiating an aura of silent power and grace.

gm-notes.notes:
Intrigue: The vendor whispers that this statue is said to come alive under the light of the full moon, offering protection to its owner or serving as a guardian against dark forces.


GM Notes (Cursed): The statue indeed animates under the full moon, but it becomes hostile and attacks the owner and anyone nearby. The curse can only be lifted by a Remove Curse spell, which will then reveal the statue’s true protective nature.

description.value:
An elaborate headdress made from vibrant bird feathers, adorned with small gems and gold accents. It is worn during important tribal ceremonies and celebrations.

Notes: This item is highly prized by local tribes and can be used to gain favor or as a significant trade item.

This rare magical fruit produces enough juice to fill a vial. Any humanoid that eats a dancing monkey fruit or drinks its juice must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or begin a comic dance that lasts for 1 minute. Humanoids that can't be poisoned are immune to this magical effect.

The dancer must use all its movement to dance without leaving its space and has disadvantage on attack rolls and Dexterity saving throws, and other creatures have advantage on attack rolls against it. Each time it takes damage, the dancer can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success. When the dancing effect ends, the humanoid suffers the poisoned condition for 1 hour.

magic-items-2.destroyFlavorText: reaches 0 charges: it crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

description.value: A sturdy container made from bamboo, perfect for storing supplies and food.

Soft, luminescent moss that grows on stones in shaded, moist areas.

It glows faintly in the dark and is often used in simple healing salves for minor wounds. This moss is used to create basic healing salves.

Marinated and grilled lizard meat, seasoned with a blend of local spices. Served on skewers and popular among street vendors.

Eating these skewers gives the consumer a mild warming sensation, protecting against the cold for 1 hour (similar to the effect of the spell Endure Elements).

magic-items-2.destroyFlavorText: reaches 0 charges: it crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

description.value:
A lengthy stright blade designed for slashing foes, the longsword is a highly versatile weapon that may also be wielded with two hands for more punishing strikes.

This item is crafted from obsidian, renowned for its razor-sharp edges. It deals an extra +1 slashing damage. Any hit with this item causes the target to suffer from bleeding, taking 1d4 damage at the start of each of its turns until it uses an action to staunch the wound. The item is lightweight, weighing 25% less than standard counterparts.

magic-items-2.destroyFlavorText: reaches 0 charges: it crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

description.value:
Mistleaf is probably the most common of all illicit substances. Relatively inexpensive and easy to grow in large fields – often by followers of the nature goddess Yondalla – Mistleaf can be found in nearly all corners of Faerun. Causing only mild addiction and adverse effects, Mistleaf can even be grown, sold and bought legally in many places, such as Luskan, Amn and Golden Fields.

While affected by Mistleaf, a user will often wear a dumbfounded expression or sheepish grin, a sign of the giddiness the herb causes. Prolonged use of Mistleaf also causes 'misty' eyes – a dull, pale sheen over the eyes, that seemingly has no effect on the user's eyesight.

Mistleaf is also known as 'Demongrass', 'Laughleaf' or 'Yondalla's tobacco'. Users often refer to the consumption of Mistleaf as 'getting misty' or 'misting'.

Dose. A pinch of dried Mistleaf. A single dose costs around 5 gp, while a bag holding 25 doses sells for around 100 gp.

Use. You can use a pipe or similar object to smoke a single dose of Mistleaf over the course of 1 minute.

Effect. 10 minutes after consuming a dose of Mistleaf, you begin to feel light and relaxed. For 1 hour, you have temporary hit points equal to your level. You also gain disadvantage on Intelligence and Wisdom ability checks.

magic-items-2.destroyFlavorText: reaches 0 charges: it crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

gm-notes.notes:
Overdosing. For each dose beyond the first, you must 
succeed on a DC 10 Constitution saving throw or become 
poisoned for the duration. If you fail this saving throw 
twice, you fall unconscious for the duration, or until you 
take damage. 


Withdrawal. When the effect ends, you must make 
a Constitution saving throw against a DC of 10 + the 
number of doses used. On a failure, you gain one level of 
exhaustion.

description.value:
A heavy metal hammer capable of being wielded with a single hand with a shield or in two hands to deliver crushing concussive blows.

This item is crafted from Dwarven Steel, known for its resilience and strength. It provides a +1 bonus to damage rolls and advantage on checks to avoid being disarmed.

description.value:
Wondrous item

This object appears as a wooden box that measures 12 inches long, 6 inches wide, and 6 inches deep. It weighs 4 pounds and floats. It can be opened to store items inside. This item also has three command words, each requiring you to use an action to speak it.

One command word causes the box to unfold into a boat 10 feet long, 4 feet wide, and 2 feet deep. The boat has one pair of oars, an anchor, a mast, and a lateen sail. The boat can hold up to four Medium creatures comfortably.

The second command word causes the box to unfold into a ship 24 feet long, 8 feet wide, and 6 feet deep. The ship has a deck, rowing seats, five sets of oars, a steering oar, an anchor, a deck cabin, and a mast with a square sail. The ship can hold fifteen Medium creatures comfortably.

When the box becomes a vessel, its weight becomes that of a normal vessel its size, and anything that was stored in the box remains in the boat.

The third command word causes the folding boat to fold back into a box, provided that no creatures are aboard. Any objects in the vessel that can't fit inside the box remain outside the box as it folds. Any objects in the vessel that can fit inside the box do so.

magic-items-2.destroyFlavorText: reaches 0 charges: it crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

description.value:
This armor consists of a coat and leggings (and perhaps a separate skirt) of leather covered with overlapping pieces of metal, much like the scales of a fish. The suit includes gauntlets.

This item is crafted from Coral Steel, a unique blend of metal and coral known for its underwater advantages and significant protection provided by its cooling effect.

The armor increases swim speed by 10 feet and provides a cooling effect that grants advantage on saving throws against heat-related conditions and exhaustion.

Additionally, it offers resistance to fire damage and provides camouflage in coral environments, granting advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks in such surroundings.

However, the rigid nature of Coral Steel imposes a -1 penalty to Dexterity checks and saving throws, and its weight is increased by 50%, reducing overall movement speed by 5 feet on land.

Extremely hot peppers native to the jungle, often used to add a fiery kick to dishes.

Eating a Fire Pepper requires a DC 14 Constitution saving throw.

Buffs and Debuffs:

  1. Success: Gain resistance to cold damage for 1 hour.
  2. Failure: Suffer disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks for 1 hour due to intense stomach pain.

This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.

This item is crafted from Dwarven Steel, known for its resilience and strength. It provides a +1 to Constitution and Strength saving throws and checks. The item is heavier, increasing its weight by 25%.

description.value:
This magnificent drum is nearly three feet in diameter, with a body made from dark, polished wood adorned with intricate gold inlays.

The drumhead is crafted from the hide of a rare jungle beast, stretched taut and painted with vibrant tribal patterns that seem to tell a story of ancient battles and rituals. The edges of the drum are adorned with colorful feathers and beads that rattle softly with every movement.

gm-notes.notes:
Intrigue: The vendor proudly claims that this drum once belonged to a great chieftain and was used in ceremonies to invoke the spirits of the jungle. It is said that those who play the drum can feel the power of the jungle coursing through them.


GM Notes (Blessed): When played, the drum grants the performer a +2 bonus to Performance checks and advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks for 1 hour. Additionally, once per day, playing the drum can inspire allies within 30 feet, granting them temporary hit points equal to the performer’s Charisma modifier.

This item is a Mastercraft weapon, forged with exceptional skill and precision. It provides a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls. The item weighs the same as standard counterparts but is of significantly higher quality, increasing its cost.

description.value:
This finely crafted leather armor shimmers with an enchanting aura, catching the eye with its ever-changing patterns and colors. It is favored by those who wish to combine protection with a flair for the dramatic.

The magic imbued within this armor allows the wearer to alter its appearance at will, transforming it into any style of clothing or armor they desire, without changing its protective properties. Whether you wish to blend in with nobility or stand out in a crowd, the Leather Armor of Glamour adapts to your needs.

  1. Armor Class (AC): 11 + Dexterity modifier
  2. Special Property: As an action, the wearer can use a command word to cause the armor to assume the appearance of any other type of clothing or armor. This illusionary appearance lasts until the wearer uses this ability again or removes the armor.
  1. Additional Notes:
  • The illusionary appearance does not change the armor's weight, feel, or actual protective capabilities.
  • The armor provides no additional benefits beyond its ability to change appearance.
  • This armor is a favorite among bards, spies, and anyone who enjoys making a statement with their attire.

description.value:
Wondrous item

This robe has cloth patches of various shapes and colors covering it. While wearing the robe, you can use an action to detach one of the patches, causing it to become the object or creature it represents. Once the last patch is removed, the robe becomes an ordinary garment.

The robe has the following patches:

  • x2 Dagger patches
  • x2 Bullseye lantern (filled and lit) patches
  • x2 Steel mirror patches
  • x2 10-foot pole patches
  • x2 Hempen rope (50 feet, coiled) patches
  • x2 Sack patches

In addition, the robe has [[/r 4d4]] other patches. The GM chooses the patches or determines them randomly from the @UUID[Compendium.dnd5e.tables.RollTable.CTGxcd51szehUGBn]{Robe of Useful Items} table.

Foundry Note

It is recommended to delete items here as necessary to track patches left.

The price guide has been calculated at 5 x item for the maximum number of items possible. Please adjust as necessary to reflect the number of patches and the DM's world economy.

description.value:
This large basin is crafted from clear, flawless crystal and is set on an ornate stand made of intertwined bronze vines and leaves. The basin is filled with water that seems to shimmer and ripple even when undisturbed.

Around the rim of the basin are engraved arcane symbols and jungle motifs, depicting scenes of prophecy and divination. The stand has small, inset gemstones that twinkle in the light.

gm-notes.notes:
• Intrigue: The vendor claims that this basin was used by a powerful shaman to see distant places and even glimpse into the future. It is said that those who peer into its depths can receive visions and guidance from the spirits of the jungle.

GM Notes (Magical): The basin functions as a scrying device. Once per day, the user can cast the Scrying spell with a success rate increased by 10%. Additionally, the user can ask one question as if using the Divination spell, with the answer appearing in the water.

description.value:
This dark, opaque bottle is sealed with a sturdy stopper, and faint wisps of smoke seem to escape from its edges. When opened, the bottle releases a thick, billowing smoke that can obscure vision and provide cover.

Unlike the traditional Ever-Smoking Bottle, this version is enchanted to provide only five uses before its magic is expended.

Mechanics:

  • Activation: As an action, the stopper can be removed, causing the smoke to pour out. The smoke quickly fills a 60-foot radius around the bottle.
  • Effect: The area becomes heavily obscured. A moderate wind (at least 10 miles per hour) can disperse the smoke in 4 rounds; a strong wind (20 miles per hour) disperses it in 1 round.
  • Duration: Once released, the smoke persists and spreads until the bottle is sealed again. The bottle can be sealed as an action, stopping the smoke.
  • Limited Uses: This version of the Ever-Smoking Bottle can be used up to five times. After the fifth use, the bottle loses its magical properties and can no longer produce smoke.

Humanoids that can’t be poisoned are immune to this magical effect.

The dried leaves of a menga bush can be ground, dissolved in a liquid, heated, and ingested. A creature that ingests 1 ounce of menga leaves in this fashion regains 1 hit point. A creature that ingests more than 5 ounces of menga leaves in a 24-hour period gains no additional benefit and must succeed on a DC 11 Constitution saving throw or fall unconscious for 1 hour. The unconscious creature awakens if it takes at least 5 damage on one turn.

A healthy menga bush usually has 2d6 ounces of leaves. Once picked, the leaves require 1 day to dry out before they can confer any benefit.
